cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the correct order of the places from east to west based on the passage. Let me start by reading the question again and the passage to make sure I understand. The question asks for the order from east to west of the four places: San Francisco, Reno, New York, Columbus. The options are given as A to D. In the passage, the author describes lining up businesses from San Francisco to New York along an imaginary road. The key points mentioned are: - Starting from San Francisco (west coast), the first noticeable change in the flagpoles is from Reno eastward. So Reno is somewhere in the west, and then moving east from Reno, the flagpoles get taller. - The flags near Columbus, Ohio are about 10 feet high, which is in the middle range (since the increase starts at Reno). Columbus is about four-fifths of the way across the nation. - As we approach the eastern terminus (New York), the poles suddenly increase in height. The largest firms are near New York,
